required_skills: Level design and layout planning for linear, open-world, or multiplayer game environments with strong spatial composition instincts
Blockout and whitebox prototyping methodology for rapidly testing spatial flow, encounter pacing, and player navigation before art production
Gameplay scripting and event sequencing using engine tools (Unreal Blueprint, Unity Visual Scripting) for triggers, spawners, cutscenes, and scripted set pieces
Player flow analysis including critical path design, optional exploration routing, and navigation clarity through environmental language
Encounter and combat space design including cover placement, engagement range management, enemy spawn logic, and arena readability
Proficiency in Unreal Engine or Unity editor for full level construction from blockout through environment art integration and final polish
Collaboration with narrative designers to embed environmental storytelling, lore objects, and scripted moments within level architecture
Playtesting and iteration methodology including self-critique, peer review, and analytics-driven redesign of underperforming level sections
preferred_skills: Multiplayer map design for competitive, co-operative, or PvP game modes with expertise in sight lines, spawn balancing, and objective placement
Open-world design systems including point of interest (POI) distribution, exploration reward pacing, and dynamic event placement
Procedural level generation systems design and scripting for roguelike, dungeon crawler, or infinite runner game formats
Performance optimization for level scenes including streaming volume setup, visibility culling, and HLOD configuration in large environments
Experience with World Partition and large world streaming in Unreal Engine 5 for seamless open-world environment delivery
Basic environment art dressing skills to self-dress blockouts with proxy assets for presentation and playtesting purposes
Shipped level design credits on at least one released game title with a demonstrable portfolio of designed levels or map layouts
responsibilities: Design and blockout level layouts using whitebox geometry that establishes spatial flow, encounter pacing, player routing, and navigational clarity before art production begins
Implement full gameplay scripting for level events including enemy spawning, trigger volumes, door sequences, puzzle logic, and cinematic moments using Blueprint or Visual Scripting
Collaborate with game designers to translate high-level encounter design briefs into playable spatial configurations that deliver intended challenge, surprise, and player satisfaction
Analyze and iterate on level design based on structured playtesting data, heatmaps, death maps, and session recordings, resolving friction points and pacing issues through redesign
Work with environment artists to guide asset placement, set dressing priorities, and visual storytelling composition within level boundaries defined during blockout
Design multiplayer maps with deliberate sight line management, balanced spawn positions, clear objective zone readability, and support for both offensive and defensive gameplay strategies
Integrate narrative and environmental storytelling elements such as readable objects, world-building props, and scripted NPC behaviors that enrich player immersion within levels
Configure level streaming, loading triggers, and World Partition cells in Unreal Engine 5 to ensure seamless performance-compliant level transitions in large open-world contexts
Maintain level design documentation including layout diagrams, scripted event flow charts, encounter spreadsheets, and iteration logs within Confluence or Notion
Participate in cross-department reviews presenting level blockouts, pacing maps, and playtest findings to leads, producers, and creative directors at defined production milestones
